title: Toulouse Capitole Paintings 🖼
excerpt: Today we went to the Toulouse Capitole. From the outside, it actually looks a bit like the White House, which makes sense because French buildings inspired a lot of famous American architecture back in the day. It has been the main heart
body:
Today we went to the Toulouse Capitole. From the outside, it actually looks a bit like the White House, which makes sense because French buildings inspired a lot of famous American architecture back in the day. It has been the main heart of Toulouse since all the way back in the 1100s. The eight big columns along the front were actually built to represent the Capitouls, who were the eight original leaders that ruled the city.

When you go inside, it stops looking like a plain old building and looks just like an actual royal palace. There are rooms and rooms of massive paintings lining the walls with gorgeous gold frames, and some are even painted right onto the ceilings! It must have taken artists years and years to finish all of this work. The big famous room called the Salle des Illustres is filled with artwork telling stories about history and mythology, which is why some of the people are naked, since that was just how classical art was done back then.

My absolute favorite painting was this super famous one done by an artist named Henri Martin. He used millions of tiny dots of paint to create the entire picture, which is just crazy looking when you see it up close! The way the sunlight lands on everyone and how the shadows were painted is so realistic. The gold frame really illuminates the sheep and makes them one of the main focuses of the whole painting. It looks like a sweet story of two people who are in love, enjoying the beautiful spring gardens, looking over their sheep together, and getting ready to spend a super happy life together. Seeing all these giant, story filled masterpieces up close was so awesome!
author: zarmina

title: Natural History Museum And Fun Exhibits 🌿
excerpt: Today we went to the Natural History Museum, where we saw this beautiful botanical garden. We explored all around and even found some cotton that Ayana stole! Then we went into the greenhouses. The tropical one was so humid that the air f
body:
Today we went to the Natural History Museum, where we saw this beautiful botanical garden. We explored all around and even found some cotton that Ayana stole! Then we went into the greenhouses. The tropical one was so humid that the air felt heavy, and there was so much moisture that water was dripping all over the plants. We also visited the desert greenhouse, which was bone dry and filled with cacti everywhere. They were all super prickly, and I was so tempted to touch them, but I resisted! We even saw a section with Venus flytraps that had a few dead flies trapped inside. I guess they were feeling hungry!

Next, we went to the minerals section and saw tons of amazing crystals and unique rocks. There was one really cool green mineral where a reaction made it look like it had bubbles all over it. Another rock was covered in sharp spikes that immediately made me think back to the cacti. We also saw a cool earthquake simulation replica. I already knew that earthquakes happen when two tectonic plates rub against each other. There were lava rocks too, which I thought were really cool. Plus a replica of the Earth showing all the different rock layers with descriptions in French. I had loads of fun exploring all the minerals, and we even got a sneak peek of the animal exhibit while we were there.

Since our tickets let us come and go whenever we pleased, we came back a little while later to properly explore the animal exhibit. We started off on the second floor, where they had realistic recreations of animals in their natural habitats. One was an otter in the water scavenging for fish on the far right side. Then we went down to the first floor and walked through a gorgeous butterfly exhibit. Mom saw a few butterflies that immediately reminded her of Africa, and they were all so pretty!

After that, we moved on to the birds and saw everything from huge feathers to the tiniest little birds and ones with the weirdest beaks. We saw an ostrich that brought back memories of a safari we went on where we saw an ostrich with an egg, and our guide asked if we wanted a massive omelet! Since we only had thirty minutes left before closing, we zoomed through the rest of the museum. We saw a bunch of cute wild cats that looked like the cat we saw on the train with the cool pattern on its coat. Then we saw some really weird rats, including one with a gross thick tail and another one that looked exactly like Peter Pettigrew from Harry Potter! We had so much fun exploring all these exhibits, and I would love to do it all over again.
author: zarmina

title: The Church near Bordeaux ⛪️
excerpt: Yesterday we went to this church on our way to Bordeaux. We were going to eat, but then we saw this humongous church, and we knew that we had to go in. The door was open, so we walked in. Outside the door, there was this amazing doorway. Th
body:
Yesterday we went to this church on our way to Bordeaux. We were going to eat, but then we saw this humongous church, and we knew that we had to go in. The door was open, so we walked in. Outside the door, there was this amazing doorway. The door was huge, and the doorway had all these little carvings on it. I didn't notice then, but each carving was very intricate and told a story of its own.

When we went inside, Puppy read us the plaque because we did not speak French, so he translated for us. We learned all about it. There was this beautiful statue, and we went into all the nooks and crannies and checked it out. There were these beautiful patterns on the walls, and I thought it looked beautiful.

Then, when I was looking around, I noticed that Mom had disappeared. This happened a lot when we were walking because she likes to take photos, which is really great. I was looking around, and they told me that she'd be out in the doorway. I looked in one doorway and I didn't see her, so I looked in the other.

That is when I noticed all the intricate designs. I noticed there were cat and dog sculpture things on the door staring at each other. There were also pigeons and other types of animals like that. There was this one thing where the design was so intricate. I imagined how hard it must have been to do such intricate details.

After that, we had some pasta at an Italian restaurant, and then we drove to Bordeaux. I definitely think if you ever go there, if you're ever in that area, you should definitely check it out.
author: ayana

title: Museum of Machines and the Minotaur
excerpt: Today we walked to the Museum of Machines, and the walk was super fun and beautiful. Our trail went right along the canal and was lined with a bunch of trees, so it was nice and shaded, which I was very grateful for! The walk was so peace
body:
Today we walked to the Museum of Machines, and the walk was super fun and beautiful. Our trail went right along the canal and was lined with a bunch of trees, so it was nice and shaded, which I was very grateful for! The walk was so peaceful because I was able to just gather my thoughts and daydream about how the rest of the day was going to go.

The moment I stepped into the exhibition area, I instantly loved it and knew it was going to be better than what I dreamed! We saw so many unique things. For example, there was a machine that literally rocked plants to sleep! There were a lot of crazy contraptions that you might think were random at first, like a machine that made an apple change colors. We even got to watch this amazing performance with a woman suspended on a rope. While hanging upside down, she used her head, arms, and legs to move through these rings made out of mist. It was crazy cool!

After that, we saw even more creations, including a machine that waters plants all by itself. Then the performer came back out, but this time the giant Minotaur from outside came indoors and she got super close to it. The Minotaur even sprayed people with water mist right out of its nostrils! Luckily we did not get wet, but near the end, we saw a guy who used a machine that shot real fire high into the air. The heat felt so intense that I cannot even imagine how hot it was for the people standing right next to it! The overall experience was so much fun, and it is definitely near the top of my list of favorites.
author: zarmina

title: Natural History Museum
excerpt: We went to a natural history museum a few days ago. We just finished going to this other museum, but it was full, so they said that the Natural History Museum would be good. Then, we walked to the Natural History Museum because it wasn't th
body:
We went to a natural history museum a few days ago. We just finished going to this other museum, but it was full, so they said that the Natural History Museum would be good. Then, we walked to the Natural History Museum because it wasn't that far.

After we got tickets, we went inside. First, we looked around this area where there were all these different things. Puppy read it aloud because it was in French, plus we don't speak French.

Next, we decided to go to the botanical gardens. We walked around for a bit. I found a cotton plant with cotton on it. So I took some of the cotton to feel how fluffy it was. It was very fluffy! While we were walking around for a little bit more, we saw this greenhouse with all these plants in it. But you could not touch the plants because they were like Venus fly traps, plus stuff like that.

Then we went to another part of another greenhouse to see all these gourds. After that, we went to a really humid greenhouse where all these rainforest plants were growing. It sounded like a rainforest, also felt like a rainforest. It was very humid with all these plants growing.

Later, we went over to a very, very hot greenhouse. It was for cacti. We didn't stay in there for long. Next, we went inside, then went into one of the exhibits on the first floor. It was an exhibit with gems plus rocks. Then, after it was finished, it caved. We walked a little bit farther until we saw an animal exhibit.

We walked around the animal exhibit for a little bit because there were so many animals. There were crocodiles, all types of birds, also a variety of different animals like goats, sheep, mammoths, foxes, bears, coyotes, dogs, cats, anteaters, armadillos, buffalo, baby elephants, koalas, sloths, plus kangaroos.

Right before, we had went to an exhibit where there was a few animals in the exhibit, but it was like a Native American exhibit where lots of things were there. But we also went to a natural disasters exhibit. We learned how an earthquake felt, plus all those kinds of stuff. We even saw a video of how volcanoes were made.
author: ayana

title: Painting Out In The Open Air 🎨🖌
excerpt: Yesterday we came upon this super cute little arts and crafts area! Even though it was closing when we first saw it, we got all the information we needed so we could go back the next day. So today, right after our chill time, we headed over
body:
Yesterday we came upon this super cute little arts and crafts area! Even though it was closing when we first saw it, we got all the information we needed so we could go back the next day. So today, right after our chill time, we headed over there. It was only a 3 minute walk from the house. The person running the place spoke very little English, but luckily another customer was able to translate for us.

We ended up doing acrylic painting, which was super different from watercolor. It was really fun looking around me to get inspiration from the flowers and animals around us. I had a bit of trouble with the body of my painting spreading too much at first, but after redoing it a few times, I finally got a shape I really liked. I filled in the background, though I decided to skip the flowers and grass since it was way too much work for the little bit of time we had left. Mom painted some flowers with a green background, but she did not really like how hers turned out. Ayana painted a dark castle and a tree with the ocean right below it, and she absolutely loved how hers turned out!

We also got to hang out with the customer who translated for us. Apparently she is a professional who creates 3D animations for a video game! Her painting was of a boy holding a star with a trail of glimmer (Ayana's idea), and she even used a template to make a cool pattern covering most of the boy's body. It was honestly one of my favorite memories in Toulouse!
author: zarmina

title: Art class🎨
excerpt: Today, Mom, Zarmina, and I went to a painting class. It was in the Royal Jardin. We were there the day before, and we decided to go that day. Puppy was doing stuff at a restaurant nearby because that was one of the only places that had
body:
Today, Mom, Zarmina, and I went to a painting class. It was in the Royal Jardin. We were there the day before, and we decided to go that day. Puppy was doing stuff at a restaurant nearby because that was one of the only places that had Wi-Fi.

When we went to the painting class, the woman there did not speak English, so she was going to ask someone for help. Then the woman who was getting supplies was like, "Oh, I know English," and she also spoke French. And so she helped us find all our stuff and get us set up with all the painting stuff. We got some frames and some paints, and we started painting.

Zarmina used one of the stencils to make a capybara because she loves capybaras. Me and Zarmina, we love capybaras. And so she kind of just copied the stencil. She didn't actually use it. She was just copying the shape of the capybara just to look at the stencil.

And then Mom, she made this beautiful flower. I love the way she did the petals and the green background. And then the person who was helping us, she made a boy catching the star. She wanted to use glitter, so I gave her the idea to make a star trail, and later I thought that she should do the bottom with glitter. The glitter added an amazing touch when I saw the finished look. I thought it looked amazing.

And I made a castle with a sea and it was a rocky cliff, but first I made the dead tree. It was a very stormy place. I really liked it. If you go to Toulouse, you should definitely try it out.
author: ayana

title: Sushi 🍣 and Cheesecake 🍰
excerpt: Today Mom, Ayana, and I went to get dinner after walking around all day at the natural history museum. The waitress asked if we would be finished by 8:30, and since it was only 7:30, she clearly did not know how fast we eat! We ordered a sh
body:
Today Mom, Ayana, and I went to get dinner after walking around all day at the natural history museum. The waitress asked if we would be finished by 8:30, and since it was only 7:30, she clearly did not know how fast we eat! We ordered a shrimp tempura appetizer that was totally amazing.

For our main course, we got a crab, avocado, and cucumber roll topped with fish eggs, a tuna, cucumber, and avocado roll with green onions on top, and a salmon and avocado roll sprinkled with sesame seeds. Everything was fabulous, and my favorite was definitely that first roll!

That was not even the best part, because we got dessert! We ordered a Japanese style lemon cheesecake, which was the most popular dessert on the menu, and it had a really good reason to be. Cheesecake crust is usually a hard graham cracker, but this one had a soft crust that still held its shape perfectly. The filling had just the right balance of lemon and creaminess. It was honestly one of the best cheesecakes I have ever had!
author: zarmina

title: We went to a museum
excerpt: We went to a museum. In the museum, there was this bull riding around. It was a humongous mechanical bull. It was really cool to watch, and it was outside. When we went inside, we saw a lot of other structures like that, like a spider, and
body:
We went to a museum. In the museum, there was this bull riding around. It was a humongous mechanical bull. It was really cool to watch, and it was outside. When we went inside, we saw a lot of other structures like that, like a spider, and then we saw one humongous spider outside, too. There was so much to do in the museum!

First, we went over to this place where lots of people were surrounding it. It was someone making fire and stuff. A few minutes before the person doing the fire thing ended, we saw someone hanging from a rope from the ceiling. It was really cool. After the fire thing ended, the person went really high up, and then another person sent rings of smoke towards them. It was a really cool performance to watch.

Then, we went to a plant area where lots of plants were getting watered and stuff like that. We checked that out for a little bit, and then we went to this movie thing upstairs. Zermina and I watched one of the movies, and we watched two boxes come up from the ground. They were holding structures like the one that we saw outside, the bull. There was a crocodile and a fish. It was really cool to see what was happening.

We stayed there for a bit longer, then Puppy said that we should go down and see the person hanging from the ceiling. We already saw that before, and it was the same person. It looked like they were getting ready for something. Then, they started playing music, and the bull that we saw from outside rode in. There were people riding inside of it, and they were pointing to the person on the ceiling. The bull sprayed water from its nose, and the person was doing all these cool tricks on the rope.

Once it was done, we were finished with the museum because we went through a lot of things, and we also saw a few knickknacks. It was amazing to see how the people designed the bull, and it was crazy how big it was. The human must have been so brave doing acrobatics on the roof like that, hanging from a rope. I would not be able to do that.

I was really glad that we went to that museum, and I would definitely go there again to see more of those things. If you go to Toulouse, you should probably see it, too!
author: ayana

title: The Park: 🦆 🪿
excerpt: Today we went to the park and came across this duck family, and they were so cute! When another duck came too close to her duckling, the mother duck instantly attacked it to protect her baby. We also saw some ducks fighting over a crab be
body:
Today we went to the park and came across this duck family, and they were so cute! When another duck came too close to her duckling, the mother duck instantly attacked it to protect her baby. We also saw some ducks fighting over a crab because they were so hungry. The ducks here searching for food by diving underwater and wiggling their butts to reach deeper in the water. Some ducks just chill on this little house made for them, while others peacefully relax on the grass, totally unbothered by anything around them. One duck even yawned and stretched its wings out at the same time, which was incredibly cute! They swim around carefully examining the water for food, but pretty much all these ducks care about is eating and sleeping. They are just terribly cute!

There were also some geese that were doing their own thing, and they were super cute too. They looked so pretty swimming around on the water. I wondered if the two geese were partners or maybe a mother and baby, since one of them was quite a bit larger than the other. They completely did their own thing, not even giving the ducks a second glance even though they were sharing the same space. The geese seemed a bit more calm and controlled, mainly spending their time sleeping. It was so much fun exploring the park in Toulouse and finding all these cute birds. It is just so awesome getting to experience new things that we would never get to see if we had stayed back in the USA!
author: zarmina

title: Museum
excerpt: We went to the center of Toulouse a few days ago, and we saw this huge museum. It looked very pretty, so we went inside of it. When we got in there, we looked around, and I saw people going into this area that I did not know what it looke
body:
We went to the center of Toulouse a few days ago, and we saw this huge museum. It looked very pretty, so we went inside of it. When we got in there, we looked around, and I saw people going into this area that I did not know what it looked like because it was covered. There was just a garden area in the front, so we decided to go where all the other people were going.

Then, when we walked in there, we saw that it was an area where you could see stairs going up to the museum part of it. We went up, and we saw these beautiful paintings on the ceilings and on the walls. They were very unique in their own way. I loved looking at them. You could look at them for hours!

When we finished going up the stairs, there were a few rooms after that. I looked at one of the rooms, and I stayed there for a little bit until I realized that there was another room. So, I went inside of that for just a tiny bit. Then I went into this other room for a little bit longer because there were so many paintings. We took photos of all the paintings. We could have definitely stayed there longer, but we had other things to do.

It was so pretty, and one of the paintings that we saw was three paintings combined. We got this little book explaining what was going on in the museum. So, I read the book and I learned all about the museum. It was a tiny little leaflet that I also used as a fan because it was super hot.

It was such a cool experience. If you go to Toulouse, you should definitely check it out.
author: ayana

title: Nutella Crepes 🥟
excerpt: While we were out exploring Carcassonne today, we stumbled upon the most amazing little crepe spot. By that point, we were getting pretty hungry, so we stopped by this super inexpensive crepe place. The second I took a bite of Mom's Nutella
body:
While we were out exploring Carcassonne today, we stumbled upon the most amazing little crepe spot. By that point, we were getting pretty hungry, so we stopped by this super inexpensive crepe place. The second I took a bite of Mom's Nutella crepe, I knew I instantly needed one for myself. We had already gotten our sandwiches, but as soon as we finished eating those, we went back a second time just to get more Nutella crepes. The guy made them so perfectly thin, soft, and easy to bite into!

By the time we finished our food and were heading out, the town had gotten super busy, so we had to weave in and out through the thick crowd. Even with all the people arriving at the end, I would definitely come back here in a heartbeat, both for those amazing Nutella crepes and to experience this whole fairytale place all over again.
author: zarmina

title: Caesar Salad🥗
excerpt: Before we started exploring Toulouse, we stopped at this super cute cafe, and I got a caesar salad that was fabulous. The chicken was not too stiff like it usually is, and the croutons, which I normally do not even like, were incredible. In
body:
Before we started exploring Toulouse, we stopped at this super cute cafe, and I got a caesar salad that was fabulous. The chicken was not too stiff like it usually is, and the croutons, which I normally do not even like, were incredible. Instead of feeling like biting into stale bread, they had such a good crunch, and everything in the salad came together so perfectly that we ended up going back there multiple times.

When we were walking around, it felt just like stepping into a fairytale. All the plants and flowers came together so beautifully, and having all the cute animals around added such a sweet touch to the whole vibe. The gorgeous water fountains also gave an extra magical feel to the whole experience. I am having so much fun looking at everything, exploring all the parks, and I am super excited to discover even more of Toulouse's beautiful places!
author: zarmina

title: Toulouse
excerpt: We are in Toulouse now, and the exact moment we walked outside, I knew it was going to be absolutely perfect. There was almost no intense heat because the humidity here is so much lower than it was in Marseille. We went to visit these gorge
body:
We are in Toulouse now, and the exact moment we walked outside, I knew it was going to be absolutely perfect. There was almost no intense heat because the humidity here is so much lower than it was in Marseille. We went to visit these gorgeous gardens, and unlike the ones in Marseille, it was not just regular plants everywhere. The colors of the flowers looked so beautiful together, and the blend of soft purples, yellowish browns, and light greens looked totally gorg. There was also a stunning fountain, and the water combined with all the flowers created the most perfect look and atmosphere. The vibe was just amazing, with everyone chilling on the grass, admiring the water fountain, and looking so grateful to be hanging out in such a beautiful place.

Then we saw all the different animals, which was so much fun. The roosters were walking around enjoying looking super superior, and there were loads of ducks that were really cute. The tiny baby ducks were eating and looking completely adorable, and there was even a majestic swan just sleeping peacefully. I even spotted a cool koi fish swimming right underneath one of the baby ducks. To make it even better, there was this total fairytale looking bridge surrounded by flowers that goes right over the river. There was also a waterfall that the pigeons absolutely loved playing in. It is just so great to be here surrounded by all this nature and positive energy.
author: zarmina

title: A Day in Carcassonne 🏰
excerpt: We went to Carcassonne a few days ago. It was a beautiful place. We went very early in the morning, and when we got there, we went into the city. We were walking around and went up to this castle. We went through this drawbridge, and it t
body:
We went to Carcassonne a few days ago. It was a beautiful place. We went very early in the morning, and when we got there, we went into the city. We were walking around and went up to this castle. We went through this drawbridge, and it turned out it was a city, Carcassonne.

It was a nice temperature. We walked around for a little bit trying to find a coffee place, and we found these beautiful areas with lots of plants. Very nice.

Then we went down to this medieval camp where we learned all these things about how people used to live back then in medieval times, and how lots of people died because of this horrible disease called the Bubonic plague, or the Black Death. We also learned about the armor. The armor was made of metal, and then there was more metal on top of it. There was just so much metal in it! We felt it, and even just a small part was so heavy. I could not imagine what it must feel like to be walking in all of that armor.

After that, we went down to a place where you could play Carcassonne in Carcassonne .

is a game. Zarmina and I played. I won, just because Puppy helped me, and it was very fun.

Then we walked around for a bit to find a place to sit and eat. We also got these amazing crepes from this guy. He made them so thin, and he did it in less than a minute, and it did not cost that much. It looked and tasted so good. He added just the right amount of stuff inside the crepe, not too much and not too little. It was delicious. We had to go back and get seconds!I loved it in Carcassonne I would love go there again to explore more.
author: ayana

title: Carcassonne 🏰
excerpt: Today we went to Carcassonne, and the moment we stepped out into the warm air, I could instantly tell it was going to be incredible. The flowers surrounding us and all the cute little houses and apartments were so pretty. When we first arri
body:
Today we went to Carcassonne, and the moment we stepped out into the warm air, I could instantly tell it was going to be incredible. The flowers surrounding us and all the cute little houses and apartments were so pretty. When we first arrived, it was so peaceful with barely anyone around. We walked across a bridge that separates the old part of town from the new part, heading toward this giant medieval fortress where people actually still live today. It looked so crazy and majestic. The second I saw it, I immediately thought it looked like something straight out of a fairytale, just like Snow White. Actually, Walt Disney got inspiration for castles like Snow White and Sleeping Beauty from places just like this!

Once we went inside the walled city, we walked past all these cute markets and little shops before heading straight to the main castle area. The inside was so beautiful, with stunning statues and detailed mosaics everywhere. Then we went up to see the view, and it was completely breathtaking. You could see endless trees, petite houses, and if you looked really far off into the distance, you could even catch a tiny glimpse of the sea. After that, we went to this history camp where we learned about how heavy the old knights' armor was, and we learned about historical diseases like the Black Death, which killed 25 million people, a massive amount of people back then.

We also got to actually play the board game Carcassonne while being right inside Carcassonne, which was so much fun! Ayana ended up winning because she had Papa on her team, which was totally unfair, but it was still such a cool memory to play the game inside the actual castle itself.
author: zarmina

title: The Duck Pond 🦆
excerpt: We went to a duck pond inside the  Royal Jardin in Toulouse   and we saw a lot of different ducks, swans, geese, and pigeons. There were all types of birds. It was really fun walking around, and then Zarmina saw this bridge, and she called
body:
We went to a duck pond inside the Royal Jardin in Toulouse and we saw a lot of different ducks, swans, geese, and pigeons.

There were all types of birds.

It was really fun walking around, and then Zarmina saw this bridge, and she called it a fairy tale bridge because it looked like something from out of a storybook.

It was really pretty.

And when we were walking, we also saw this amazing sparkling waterfall, and we went across a bridge and saw some little ducklings.

There were two baby ducklings.

They were so cute and so small.

I loved looking at them.

They were just swimming around and eating stuff.

And then Zarmina noticed that there were fishes in the pond.

It was a very cool experience.

We went back there like two other times, and I really liked it.
author: ayana

title: Fashion Exhibit
excerpt: T oday we went to a museum and got to see this super cool fashion exhibit. I learned so much about sewing and how designers create all these different styles and textures in clothing. Most of the signs and descriptions were in French so I c
body:
T

oday we went to a museum and got to see this super cool fashion exhibit. I learned so much about sewing and how designers create all these different styles and textures in clothing. Most of the signs and descriptions were in French so I could not understand every single word, but I learned that the exhibit was all about a famous French designer named Mossi Traore. He is incredibly creative and uses his work to bring together all kinds of different cultures. It was fascinating to see how he designed actual soccer jerseys for teams and then turned right around to make stunning dresses for models to wear on the runway. He loves to draw inspiration from diverse heritages, especially Indian culture and traditional garments, and he blends those beautiful textile crafts into his modern masterpieces. It is so cool how he shapes these complex outfits just by draping and folding pieces of cloth together before sewing them up.

Even though some of the clothes and dresses he made were not exactly my personal fashion taste, it was still amazing to learn about how clothing is constantly evolving around the world. It really showed me how many unique styles and traditions of fashion exist across different cultures. Learning about all of this made me feel so much more appreciative of design, and it makes me super excited to discover even more unique types of fashion as we keep traveling to new places.
author: zarmina

title: A Day at the Mucem!
excerpt: During our time in Marseille, we decided to visit the Mucem. The moment we arrived, we passed by the main entrance for a bit and walked up to a beautiful, sun-drenched garden area overlooking the sea. We spent some time wandering through th
body:
During our time in Marseille, we decided to visit the Mucem. The moment we arrived, we passed by the main entrance for a bit and walked up to a beautiful, sun-drenched garden area overlooking the sea. We spent some time wandering through the greenery, soaking in the views, before heading down to the main ticketing area. After scanning our options, we decided to get tickets for a fashion exhibition that was currently on display.

As we stepped into the exhibit and began looking around, a specific display caught my eye: a large TV screen showcasing footage of a high-fashion runway show. I walked over to watch it closely. Some of the outfits were incredibly cute, and I found myself genuinely loving the designs. But sometimes the outfits were not my style. However, what fascinated me the most wasn't the clothing—it was the models' faces. They walked with absolute intensity, completely devoid of any emotion or expression. And some of the faces looked really funny to me.

After we were done watching the runway , we spent a little more time walking through the rest of the exhibit. We got to see the behind-the-scenes process and learned a lot about how the clothes were actually made, which was incredibly cool. Some of the more designs weren't really my style, and I didn't love every single piece on display. Still, the overall experience was fantastic. If you ever find yourself at the Mucem, I would definitely sa y you should go check it out!
author: ayana

title: Street paintings !🎨
excerpt: In the morning, I went to this place filled with street art called La Panier. We saw all this beautiful art and a couple of dogs. We walked around and saw beautiful paintings everywhere, and we kept walking for a pretty long time. I didn't
body:
In the morning, I went to this place filled with street art called La Panier. We saw all this beautiful art and a couple of dogs. We walked around and saw beautiful paintings everywhere, and we kept walking for a pretty long time.

I didn't put on any sunscreen because it was pretty foggy and I thought we weren't going to stay for that long. But we ended up staying for like a few hours!

Then we saw this beautiful painting that had a door and some flowers out of a window. My dad took a photo of me in three places. It was really fun for me to act out those three things—it was because of a filter that he had on his phone, and it made it look like I was painted everywhere. There was even a cute little cat in the corner.

We saw a lot of other fun paintings, like a painting of a woman, a rat, and a lot of paintings of cats. We also saw these cute little alleys with lots of bushes and a few trees.

Then we saw this place where it looked like there used to be a painting class, because there were photos hung over the wall. It was like we were walking through an art gallery. Some of the paintings were abstract and some of them were realistic. It was really fun walking through there, so I took a video of all the paintings with my dad's phone.

I would definitely like to go there again. Maybe a little bit earlier, so that we can check out some more of those paintings.
author: ayana

title: Yum! 🫔
excerpt: While we were walking around and exploring, I came across this amazing pastry shop that my dad had actually tried before and highly recommended. It was seriously so good, with sweet sugar sprinkled all over the outside and a rich, delicious
body:
While we were walking around and exploring, I came across this amazing pastry shop that my dad had actually tried before and highly recommended. It was seriously so good, with sweet sugar sprinkled all over the outside and a rich, delicious cream filling on the inside. I happily ate it while strolling through the streets, and at one point, I even stopped to watch a few dogs playing off leash together and having the absolute best time. It was cute! (And I was thinking about Ruby a lot after that.) It is just so much fun to travel to France, discover new things about the world, and learn something new every single day. These experiences are truly incredible, and they make me feel so grateful and compassionate toward the world around me.

Since this was all part of the same awesome day, I was also constantly looking up at the beautiful graffiti and street art covering the walls. It is honestly mind blowing how these artists can create such highly complicated and detailed pieces in public spaces. The murals look like they require so much talent and planning, yet the artists make the whole process look completely effortless. Seeing all this creativity mixed with the lively streets, the delicious food, and the happy local dogs just made me feel so inspired and connected to this beautiful place.
author: zarmina

title: Street Art 🖌
excerpt: Today we went exploring the streets where the street art is super famous, and it was so cool to see how all these different artists express themselves. Out of all the paintings we saw, this one really pretty piece of a little girl absolut
body:
Today we went exploring the streets where the street art is super famous, and it was so cool to see how all these different artists express themselves. Out of all the paintings we saw, this one really pretty piece of a little girl absolutely caught my eye. She is standing there so gently cradling a tiny hedgehog in her hands, and she looks completely focused on keeping it safe. Because almost the entire painting is done in simple black and white, it really highlights how peaceful and sweet the moment is, making you focus entirely on her and the little creature.

What makes the painting stand out even more is the bright red color of her dress and the flower in her hair. Since everything else is in black and white, that sudden splash of red is super important because it expresses so much emotion and instantly draws your eyes right to her. The red really shows a sense of warmth, love, and protection, which perfectly matches how carefully she is holding the tiny hedgehog. It completely brings the whole painting to life and makes the connection she has with the hedgehog feel incredibly strong and special.

We also found some more incredible street art that absolutely blew me away. In the right hand corner, there was this cute little cat painted. Right next to it there is a door and it literally looks like I am fading directly into the painting, but really I was just trying to yank the door open. (Nobody lives there.) Then on the left hand side, they had a bunch of pots that were not actually potted at all because they were cut in half and glued straight onto the windows, which was such a cool and creative idea.

Seeing all of this has made such a massive impact on me. Getting to experience all this new art has really opened my eyes, and I feel like I have so much more appreciation for different styles now. I just love looking at things like this because they are so unique and make exploring the city streets so much more exciting.
author: zarmina

title: Palais des Papes
excerpt: Today we went to Avignon, which is this amazing city in southern France, and we visited the Palais des Papes. That translates to the Palace of the Popes, and it is basically where the Pope used to do absolutely everything. For thousands of
body:
Today we went to Avignon, which is this amazing city in southern France, and we visited the Palais des Papes. That translates to the Palace of the Popes, and it is basically where the Pope used to do absolutely everything. For thousands of years, the Pope had always been based in Rome in Italy, but back in the year 1309, Rome became incredibly dangerous. A French Pope decided he had officially had enough of all that chaos, so he moved the whole operation to Avignon. In total, seven popes ended up living and ruling from here for nearly seventy years.

The palace itself is just stunning. It is absolutely massive, and the main courtyard is so giant that it can easily hold thousands of spectators at once. What is wild is how fast they built it. It was the ultimate construction project of the fourteenth century, and they finished the main parts in just twenty years. Because the popes accumulated so much wealth from all over Europe, they kept it hidden in secret vaults beneath the stone floors so that thieves and raiders could never find it. They also had this legendary kitchen with an enormous funnel shaped ceiling that looks like a single giant chimney. It is so ridiculously big that you feel like actual giants could live there.

Since the popes were constantly paranoid about being attacked by bandits or enemy armies, they constructed the palace to double as a major military stronghold. There are defenses built in everywhere. Those little crosses you see in the stone walls are actually arrow slits, which let archers and crossbowmen shoot at enemies while staying completely safe. While they did not have bullets back in the 1300s, they had special floor openings at the top of the walls where defenders could drop massive rocks or boiling liquids on anyone trying to get too close.

Being there was an incredibly epic experience, especially because we got to visit during the famous Avignon Festival. The entire place was buzzing with energy, and there were so many street performers doing wild stunts and tricks everywhere, all trying to persuade you to come to their shows. It was incredibly hot outside, and there were so many people packed into the narrow dining streets, restaurants, and cafes. Even though we were totally melting in the crowd, it was so much fun to experience the whole thing.
author: zarmina

title: Stunts!
excerpt: Today, we went to a town called Avignon. We were gonna see a dance performance later that day, but sadly we could not go because it was so hot, so we had time to explore. It honestly felt like I was melting in the sun! Even though we couldn
body:
Today, we went to a town called Avignon. We were gonna see a dance performance later that day, but sadly we could not go because it was so hot, so we had time to explore. It honestly felt like I was melting in the sun!

Even though we couldn't make it to our original plans, when we were walking around, we saw this open area where these people were doing amazing stunts. I would have never known that anybody could do things like that! They were doing massive backflips high up in the air, and then the other people were catching them perfectly on the way down.

They kept doing crazy stunts, eventually standing right on top of each other and making something like a giant pyramid thing. It was so cool to watch them balance. After they finished, my mom decided to give the people a tip. I went over and walked with her to drop it off. The whole experience was just so cool, and it was amazing how flexible they all were. I definitely want to watch them again!
author: ayana

title: Street Dancing 💃
excerpt: Today we saw so much amazing street dancing. There were tons of live shows happening, and the performers were doing some incredible stunts to persuade people to come check out their main acts. The dancers were just so talented, pulling off
body:
Today we saw so much amazing street dancing. There were tons of live shows happening, and the performers were doing some incredible stunts to persuade people to come check out their main acts. The dancers were just so talented, pulling off flawless handstands, aerials, and even full backflips right there on the pavement.

At the same time, it was also SOO hot outside. Everyone was feeling super dehydrated and tired, especially me. I was honestly melting, but we managed to stay in the shade quite a bit, and luckily the worst of the actual heat wave had already passed before we got there. Even so, the temperatures were easily reaching the 100s and 120s. It felt like a massive, high energy outdoor concert, and despite the intense weather, the sheer talent of the dancers made the whole experience totally worth it.
author: zarmina

title: The Cathédrale Sainte-Marie-Majeure
excerpt: A super long time ago, there was an old church in the exact same spot called the Old Major. When they wanted to build a new, much bigger one in the 1850s, the builders were going to smash the old one completely! But the people who l
body:
A super long time ago, there was an old church in the exact same spot called the Old Major. When they wanted to build a new, much bigger one in the 1850s, the builders were going to smash the old one completely! But the people who lived in Marseille got really mad and yelled at them not to do it. Because of that, they actually saved a piece of the old church, and you can still see it sitting right next to the giant new one today.

The guy who laid the very first stone for the new church was actually Emperor Napoleon III! Back then, Marseille was making a ton of money from all their trading boats, so they wanted a gigantic church to show off how rich they were. It is so huge that 3,000 people can fit inside at the same time.

Because it is so big, it took forty whole years and three different architects to finally finish it. My favorite part is how it looks on the outside. It has giant stripes everywhere! They made the stripes by using green stones all the way from Florence and white stones from a quarry nearby. The architects mixed different styles together to make it look like a mix of Europe and the East.

For a long time, it was kind of hard to walk up to the cathedral because there were too many cars and big port buildings in the way. But recently, they built cool underground tunnels to hide all the traffic! Now there is a huge, beautiful walkway right by the water. The wind from the Mediterranean Sea is super salty and damages the rocks sometimes, so they always have to put scaffolding up to fix it and keep it looking nice. I think it is one of the coolest building in Marseille!
author: ayana

title: Cathédrale de la Major
excerpt: We all went to Cathédrale de la Major! It was beautiful and had amazing massive domes, gorgeous marble altars, and stunning mosaics inside. There was so much history and a story behind the whole structure. It was built back in the 1800s, an
body:
We all went to Cathédrale de la Major! It was beautiful and had amazing massive domes, gorgeous marble altars, and stunning mosaics inside. There was so much history and a story behind the whole structure. It was built back in the 1800s, and Emperor Napoleon III actually laid the very first stone right by the sea to welcome people arriving on boats.

The brains behind it was an architect named Henri Jacques Espérandieu, who is the very same architect behind Notre Dame de la Garde, which you have seen in some of my other blog posts. This building is super huge and I couldn't believe it can hold 3,000 people at once! Walking up to it, the vibe is just so grand and magnificent. The outside has these awesome stripes made of beautiful local white Cassis stone mixed with dark green marble brought all the way from Italy, which gives the whole place a cool, unique Middle Eastern look. Inside, I was able to see the incredible stained glass windows, and they are so beautiful! The pictures in the stained glass represent colorful art and classic stories that light up when the sun shines through. I also looked around to see if there was any military stuff inside, but it turns out the military models and ships are actually up at Notre Dame de la Garde. La Major is instead super famous for its massive, detailed mosaics on the floor!

It is also super cool to see how the building evolved over time to show off its religious history. Since it is a Catholic cathedral built right by the sea, religion totally affected the design and gave it a beautiful mix of different styles. When you look up at the ceilings and around the altars, the whole place literally glitters because there is so much shiny gold and gorgeous artwork everywhere. All of the gold and holy decorations give the inside such an amazing and peaceful vibe, showing how much care went into making it a sacred space.

Also, talk about out with the old! This giant place was actually built on the exact site of an ancient fifth century church. If you walk around the side, you can still see a small remaining portion of that original old church left standing. Old medieval churches like that give off such a cool vibe, and seeing the historic ruins attached to the big new cathedral was really cute and stuff. It adds so much history and an extra little story to our whole day out. Between the incredible views outside and the beautiful art inside, we had another amazing adventure today!
author: zarmina

title: Day trip 🌴
excerpt: Today we went to the sea. The beach was super rocky! It was basically a free foot massage that absolutely nobody asked for, but thankfully, the water was super clear where we were. If you look closely in the water, you'll see a rock that's
body:
Today we went to the sea. The beach was super rocky! It was basically a free foot massage that absolutely nobody asked for, but thankfully, the water was super clear where we were. If you look closely in the water, you'll see a rock that's where Ayana, Mom, and I swam over. I saw the other side of the water and it was icky, so I'm very grateful that we had clean water on our side!

After the cove, we migrated over to a crepe shop because it was really hot. I got a Nutella crepe with bananas that I shared with Ayana, Mom got a citrus one, and Papa got a brown sugar one. They all were really good, and it was the perfect sweet treat to end the heat! Between the swimming adventure and the delicious food, I had the most amazing day today!
author: zarmina

title: The Beach!🏖️
excerpt: Today I went to a beach. In the car, we were just deciding whether we would go to a nice and sandy beach where we could swim, or go to a beautiful beach with all these cliffs and flowers and plants. It would be really pretty, and so we deci
body:
Today I went to a beach. In the car, we were just deciding whether we would go to a nice and sandy beach where we could swim, or go to a beautiful beach with all these cliffs and flowers and plants. It would be really pretty, and so we decided to go to that beach even though the rocks would be kind of uncomfortable.

So, when we got there, Mom went up to see the view, and Puppy laid down on the towels on top of the rocks 'cause there wasn't really any sand. Zarmina and I tried to go all the way in. We waited for a few minutes to get used to the water, then I went all the way in. Sometimes I went really far and swam close to the buoys, but sometimes I just stayed near Zarmina in the shallow end of the water.

When Mom came back, she said the view wasn't that great. Then Mom and I swam to the big rock in the middle. Surrounding the rock were other smaller, mossy green rocks that we could stand on. It was nice. We stayed there for a little bit, but then we went into town.

Overall, I had a pretty fun experience, but I'd probably go to a different, more sandy beach next time.
author: ayana

title: Treats!🍰
excerpt: Today we drove to a cute little town and went for a walk. During our walk, we saw this amazing bakery filled with glazed treats. They looked perfected with strawberries, raspberries, and all kinds of fruits! I was having trouble deciding w
body:
Today we drove to a cute little town and went for a walk. During our walk, we saw this amazing bakery filled with glazed treats. They looked perfected with strawberries, raspberries, and all kinds of fruits!

I was having trouble deciding which one I would get because they all looked so delicious. Zarmina finally chose hers, but I was still deciding between two. One was a strawberry cake slice that looked amazing. The other had custard cream on the bottom with raspberries and powdered sugar sprinkled over it. It looked so good!

In the end, I decided on the strawberry cake slice, and it was delicious. It was nice and creamy and had strawberries inside of it. It was really good, and I had such a fun time there at the bakery. All the food looked so good.

I even took a bite of Zarmina's and Puppy's treats. They were both really delicious, and I would love to go to that place again and try everything. The store looks so nice!
author: ayana

title: Desserts 😋
excerpt: Today I was looking for desserts and we came upon this shop. The desserts that we got were so good and incredible! I had a type of cream filling in there, with whipped cream on top, sandwiched in between two pieces of bread. It was so goo
body:
Today I was looking for desserts and we came upon this shop.

The desserts that we got were so good and incredible!

I had a type of cream filling in there, with whipped cream on top, sandwiched in between two pieces of bread.

It was so good!

Ayana had some strawberry cake, a cheesecake and it was super good as well.

Papa had this miniature crème brûlée that was super cute and super good!

Anyways, it was all really good and delicious.

We had so much fun today exploring and eating in Aix!
author: zarmina

title: The Musuem
excerpt: Today I went to a museum and saw a cave exhibit. We learned that thousands of years ago, the water level was 400 feet shallower than it is now. This was because there were glaciers everywhere back then, but today there are almost no glacier
body:
Today I went to a museum and saw a cave exhibit. We learned that thousands of years ago, the water level was 400 feet shallower than it is now. This was because there were glaciers everywhere back then, but today there are almost no glaciers in the world.

I also learned that a man named Henry Cosquer went into an underwater cave and discovered old caveman paintings from thousands of years ago. There were also many marks from a fire. At the museum, we saw a version of the cave that wasn't underwater. Scientists remade the cave so that we could see what it looked like from the inside.

Some of the caveman paintings he saw were of seals, cave lions, and penguins. There were also some pictures of bison and the ibex goat, but mostly there were horses. It is crazy to see how thousands of years ago was so much different than it is today.
author: ayana

title: Boat ride!⛴️
excerpt: Today, I went on a boat. While we were out there, I saw beautiful, luscious green mountains with lots of forests and bushes. There was shining blue water beneath me. I was in the front of the boat when a small, little leafy green butterfl
body:
Today, I went on a boat. While we were out there, I saw beautiful, luscious green mountains with lots of forests and bushes. There was shining blue water beneath me. I was in the front of the boat when a small, little leafy green butterfly landed on my arm, right in between my bracelet and my rubber band.

When it first landed, it looked like it was going to suck the blood out of me! But Puppy wanted to take a photo of it, so I stopped trying to shake it off because I realized it was a pretty butterfly. I looked at it closely and saw how much it looked like a leaf. One of its wings was smaller and a lighter green, and the other wing was a slightly darker green.

It was a really pretty butterfly, but after a few minutes, it flew away from my arm. It tickled! When it left, I asked myself: was it there to take a rest on me, or did it think that I was a branch of a tree?
author: ayana

title: Boat Trip
excerpt: Today we were able to go on a boat! It was a 3-hour adventure and we learned about the islands all around. We even got to see a nature preserve for birds. The water was a lot cleaner than the beach that we went to. Even though we didn't g
body:
Today we were able to go on a boat!

It was a 3-hour adventure and we learned about the islands all around.

We even got to see a nature preserve for birds.

The water was a lot cleaner than the beach that we went to.

Even though we didn't get to go in the water, you could sense and see that the water was in pristine condition.

Mom and I had a bit of seasickness, but the view of Marseille, the mountains, and the little coves was amazing!
author: zarmina

title: Notre Dame de la Garde
excerpt: Today I went to Notre-Dame de la Garde on a mini train. The train gave cool facts about the places around us while we traveled all the way up to the structure. It's famous for the statue on top, which is a mother and baby holding each other
body:
Today I went to Notre-Dame de la Garde on a mini train.

The train gave cool facts about the places around us while we traveled all the way up to the structure.

It's famous for the statue on top, which is a mother and baby holding each other, expressing a mother's love for her offspring.

From up there, I also saw this beautiful view surrounding Notre-Dame de la Garde.

This view shows Marseille with all the buildings and boats with a little bit of the sea!
author: zarmina

title: Cave Exploration
excerpt: We went to this museum-like place by accident because we thought it was a different museum (the one right next to it). We got to watch a video where it explained, with no words, that the water rose over what used to be the ground in -3300.
body:
We went to this museum-like place by accident because we thought it was a different museum (the one right next to it). We got to watch a video where it explained, with no words, that the water rose over what used to be the ground in -3300. It showed how the cave people would make drawings in the cave, which was a sacred place. No one slept there because it was so important.

We got an audio guide to explain what was going on in the replica of the cave. It talked about the many different drawings made with charcoal, and how the cave people drew animals like horses, penguins, megaceros (deer with huge antlers), ibex, bison, and lions. Right after learning about the drawings, we got to see life-like models of the animals! The bison was huge I couldn't believe that they used to kill them and the seal had the biggest eyes!

The guide also explained the finger and handprints, where they used charcoal to fill in the spaces in between and around the fingers in a circle shape. Some of the fingers were stubs, which could mean that they bent them over or cut them off and lost them somehow.

After that, we looked at the cool stalagmites that were formed over time when the water started to erase some of the limestone, making these skinny, stick-like structures. I also saw some that looked like chandeliers because they were formed on an arch, making them upside down.

On that happy note, it was so much fun going to the cave even though it was the wrong thing. We really had an adventure!
author: zarmina

title: Ice Cream 🍦
excerpt: Ice cream parlors were everywhere, but we decided on Mustache and I actually ended up getting a mustache! I chose the strawberry cheesecake flavor. After that, the journey to get Ayana shorts was difficult because the heat just kept climb
body:
Ice cream parlors were everywhere, but we decided on Mustache and I actually ended up getting a mustache!

I chose the strawberry cheesecake flavor.

After that, the journey to get Ayana shorts was difficult because the heat just kept climbing!
author: zarmina

title: Bakery!🥐
excerpt: Today I was walking around on a street with my family. We had just finished getting these creamy, nice smoothies. We were finishing them off, but my dad was hungry, so we went to this bakery. We saw this amazing, perfected crois
body:
Today I was walking around on a street with my family.

We had just finished getting these creamy, nice smoothies.

We were finishing them off, but my dad was hungry, so we went to this bakery.

We saw this amazing, perfected croissant—every detail was perfect.

There was no one in the store except for the workers, so we decided to go in and get the croissant.

After we bought it, I tried it, and it was fluffy and creamy.

It was so nice, and I was very happy that we got it.
author: ayana

title: Beach
excerpt: This is the usual number of people on the beach in this weather! It’s been so hot here lately that I think literally the entire city decided to sow up at the exact same spot today! This isn't even as many people on a beach as I've seen!
body:
This is the usual number of people on the beach in this weather!

It’s been so hot here lately that I think literally the entire city decided to sow up at the exact same spot today!

This isn't even as many people on a beach as I've seen!
author: zarmina

title: The Eiffel Tower
excerpt: Today I saw the Eiffel Tower with my Dad on a boat.
body: Today I saw the Eiffel Tower with my Dad on a boat.
author: ayana

title: Renoir exhibit
excerpt: The exhibit showed beautiful paintings. There was history and a story behind every painting and every technique, and I was able to listen to an audio to find out more about them. For example, this painting has little intricate brushstrokes
body:
The exhibit showed beautiful paintings.

There was history and a story behind every painting and every technique, and I was able to listen to an audio to find out more about them.

For example, this painting has little intricate brushstrokes which are very important to the painting.

Since it's Impressionism, you aren't meant to see the exact details of everything only the idea of the shape.
author: zarmina

title: The three littles are there if you can see them
excerpt: The three littles are there if you can see them!
body: The three littles are there if you can see them!
author: adnaniftekhar

title: First breakfast in Paris
excerpt: First breakfast in Paris! Adnan and Amy went grocery shopping before the girls got up. Staying in our cute little airbnb in the 17th. The local bakeries are amazing and it was fun trying to grocery shop in Paris again. The fruit is super e
body:
First breakfast in Paris!

Adnan and Amy went grocery shopping before the girls got up.

Staying in our cute little airbnb in the 17th.

The local bakeries are amazing and it was fun trying to grocery shop in Paris again.

The fruit is super expensive but so yummy.

Coffee so far is ... meh.
author: adnaniftekhar

title: First full day in Paris
excerpt: First full day in Paris!
body: First full day in Paris!
author: adnaniftekhar

title: Dinner a few hours after arriving
excerpt: Dinner a few hours after arriving. The clouds are amazing and so glad we got a chance to walk into Montmartre! And, it's not hot - so pleasant the first few days!
body:
Dinner a few hours after arriving.

The clouds are amazing and so glad we got a chance to walk into Montmartre!

And, it's not hot - so pleasant the first few days!
author: adnaniftekhar
